meta.title: Resources & Learning Center | Prosperity Planning & Advisory
meta.description: Financial tools, guides, calculators, and educational resources from Prosperity Planning & Advisory in Woodland Hills, CA. Explore retirement, tax, investment, and planning resources.
meta.ogTitle: Resources & Learning Center | Prosperity Planning & Advisory
meta.ogDescription: Financial tools, guides, and educational resources to help you make more confident financial decisions.

hero.headline: Financial Tools, Guides & Educational Resources
body: At Prosperity Planning and Advisory, we believe informed decisions lead to better outcomes — but clarity comes from understanding how those decisions fit into your full financial life.

This Resources & Learning Center is designed to give individuals, families, small business owners, and professionals practical tools and educational resources to help you explore important financial decisions with greater confidence.

These tools are not just here to provide numbers — they are here to help you think more clearly, ask better questions, and take the next step with purpose. These materials are provided for general educational purposes only and are not personalized investment, tax, or legal advice.

startHere.heading: Start Here
startHere.intro: Not sure where to begin? Use this quick guide:

items.action: Start with the Capital Gains Calculator
Use the Investment or Roth IRA Calculator
Use the Cost of Care Tool

howToUse.heading: How to Use This Page
howToUse.intro: These resources are designed to help you explore financial decisions — not replace a comprehensive plan.
howToUse.listHeading: To get the most value from these tools:
items: Focus on what the results mean, not just the numbers
Think about how each decision connects to your overall plan
howToUse.footnote: Calculators are most powerful when used as part of a coordinated strategy across investments, taxes, retirement, and cash flow. Use of this page does not create an advisory relationship, and the information here is not tailored to your individual circumstances.

tools.title: Capital Gains Tax Calculator
tools.helpsWith: Estimate taxes when selling appreciated assets
tools.whyMatters: Understand how taxes may affect net proceeds before acting
tools.usedWhen: Evaluating asset sales, comparing scenarios, and preparing for conversations with your tax and financial professionals
tools.note: Results are hypothetical estimates based on your inputs and should not be relied on alone for tax decisions. Strategies involving capital gains should be coordinated with your CPA or tax professional.

tools.title: Charitable Remainder Unitrust Calculator
tools.helpsWith: Estimates income and charitable outcomes of a CRUT strategy
tools.whyMatters: Helps explore charitable, income, and legacy planning concepts
tools.usedWhen: Reviewing highly appreciated asset scenarios and charitable planning ideas
tools.note: A charitable remainder unitrust is an advanced strategy involving legal and tax considerations. Results are hypothetical estimates and should be reviewed with your attorney, CPA, and financial advisor before implementation.

tools.title: Investment Calculator
tools.whyMatters: Illustrates the impact of compounding and consistent saving
tools.usedWhen: Goal setting, contribution planning, and long-term projections
tools.note: Calculator outputs are hypothetical projections based on assumptions you enter and are not guarantees of future investment performance or results.

tools.title: Roth IRA Calculator
tools.helpsWith: Estimates potential Roth IRA growth and future tax-free income concepts
tools.whyMatters: Helps evaluate retirement savings and tax diversification ideas
tools.usedWhen: Comparing retirement account strategies and long-term income planning
tools.note: Results are hypothetical estimates only. Eligibility, tax treatment, and contribution strategies depend on your individual circumstances and should be reviewed with your tax professional.

tools.title: Annuity Calculator
tools.helpsWith: Estimates income or value from annuity scenarios
tools.whyMatters: Helps explore one possible approach to retirement income planning
tools.usedWhen: Reviewing longevity income needs and comparing income approaches
tools.note: Annuities are one option among many and should be evaluated in light of fees, liquidity, surrender terms, guarantees, and your broader financial plan. Calculator results are estimates only and are not a recommendation or endorsement of any product.

tools.title: Net Price Calculator
tools.helpsWith: Estimates net college costs after aid
tools.whyMatters: Helps families plan more realistically for education expenses
tools.note: Actual aid packages, school policies, and institutional calculations can vary. This tool provides an estimate only.

tools.title: Mortgage Calculator
tools.whyMatters: Helps evaluate affordability and cash flow impact
tools.usedWhen: Reviewing home purchase and refinancing scenarios
tools.note: Actual loan terms, taxes, insurance, rates, and lender requirements will vary. This calculator provides an estimate only and should be reviewed alongside your full financial picture.

tools.title: Cost of Long-Term Care Calculator
tools.helpsWith: Estimates long-term care costs by location
tools.whyMatters: Highlights potential future care-related financial risks
tools.usedWhen: Starting retirement protection and aging-planning discussions
tools.note: Costs and care needs vary widely by region, provider, and level of care. This tool is intended as a general estimate only.

prosperityInsight.heading: Prosperity Insight
paragraphs: Many financial mistakes happen when decisions are made in isolation rather than as part of a coordinated plan.
These tools are most powerful when used together — as part of a coordinated financial plan that considers taxes, investments, income, and long-term goals.

howAndWhy.heading: How and Why to Use These Tools
paragraphs: Financial calculators provide estimates, but they don't account for the full picture.
Most tools focus on one decision at a time, while real financial planning connects multiple areas together.
items: Use results to guide better decisions, not replace them
howAndWhy.closing: A calculator gives you data. A financial plan helps put that data into context.
howAndWhy.cta: Need help interpreting your results? If you'd like personalized guidance, schedule a Clarity Call.

topics.title: Family & Education Planning
topics.description: Support for college planning, savings strategies, and family financial decisions.

topics.title: Estate & Legacy Planning
topics.description: Educational content around wealth transfer, beneficiary alignment, and legacy goals.

topics.title: Small Business Planning
topics.description: Resources for business owners focused on cash flow, retirement, and tax-aware strategies.

topics.title: Attorney-Specific Planning Resources
topics.description: Focused planning considerations for attorneys and high-income professionals.

educationalNote:
These calculators and third-party resources are provided for general educational purposes only and are intended to help you explore financial concepts. They are not personalized recommendations, are not based on your individual circumstances, and should not be relied upon as the sole basis for any financial decision.

Results are hypothetical estimates based on the assumptions entered and are not guarantees of future outcomes, investment performance, lending terms, tax treatment, insurance results, or eligibility for any program or benefit. Actual outcomes will vary.

Links to third-party websites are provided as a convenience only. They do not imply Prosperity Planning and Advisory's endorsement, approval, affiliation, or responsibility for the content, calculations, privacy practices, or accuracy of any external site.

Use of these resources does not create an advisory relationship. Prosperity Planning and Advisory does not provide legal or tax advice. You should consult your CPA, attorney, or other qualified professional regarding tax, legal, or other specialized matters.

closing.heading: Turn Information Into a Personalized Plan
paragraphs: Online tools are a great starting point — but clarity comes from understanding how each decision fits into your full financial life.
If appropriate for your situation, personalized guidance can help you evaluate tradeoffs, avoid costly mistakes, and build a strategy aligned with your goals.
